Mtn To Compel Depo Of Deft 99 Cents Only Stores & Req For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-18-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Deposition of Defendant 99 Cents Only Stores is granted as it seeks to compel the deposition. The deposition shall proceed on May 23, 2005 as offered by Defendant. The moving party does not address the objections to the notice of deposition, and the court does not address them in this motion. However, the parties should be guided by the ruling made on May 13, 2005. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF RUSSELL BRIMER'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Things, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant 99 Cents Only Store And Its Counsel IS OFF CALENDAR TO BE RE-NOTICED IN THE DISCOVERY DEPT. (301/JLW/) Reporter: CHRISTINA PAXTON CSR#1558

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Further Reponses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Things, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant 99 Cents Only Store And Its Counsel. (Set In Error In Dept. 301).
Off Calendar May-13-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Daniel Bornstein, for Plaintiff and moving party Russell Brimer
Patrick Cafferty, for Defendant and opposing party 99 Cents Only Stores
George Schumer, CSR. #3326

Ruling:
Argued and Tentative Ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Further Responses To Requests For Production of Documents and Things, For Production Of Documents and Things is moot in part and denied in part. As supplemental responses have been served, the motion is moot. The court will not determined the adequacy of the supplemental responses on this motion except as set forth.
The definition of products is overly broad. Without addressing the Health and Safety Code's requirements, Plaintiff's assertion that there is an obligation to investigate possible violations by testing all glassware with colored artwork or designs is incorrect as a matter of discovery law.
The court finds that the responses are adequate as they respond that no non-privileged investigative activities have taken place. While Plaintiff must respond as to identified products as Plaintiff has knowledge of the existence of lead and/or cadium, Plaintiff is not obligated by the notice or discovery requests to perform tests on all glassware with colored artwork or designs.
The court cannot compel a further response where Defendant says it has no knowledge. Defendant is not required by the discovery code to conduct tests of such glassware to determine whether there is lead and/or cadium. If Plaintiff believes that there is an obligation to conduct the testing it urges by this motion, that is a matter to be argued to the trier of fact at the trial of the matter. If Defendant has given a response that asserts that no investigation has taken place, that is sufficient for that purpose. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-22-2005 Set For Trial On Aug-29-05 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Aug-11-05 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 303
